_raw_search_text: {"success": true, "result": "SearchResponse(request_id='9586cf32-d26b-4c31-8c3d-92afb4ba9a6e', results=[Result(content='', description='3 days ago \u2014 Is HubSpot hiring now? Yes, HubSpot has 35 open jobs. \u00b7 Is it hard to get a job at HubSpot? People have reported that the interview at HubSpot is difficult.', metadata=ResultMetadataSerpMetadata(country='US', entity_type='OrganicResult', locale='en', position=1, driver='vx10-pro'), title='HubSpot Careers and Employment', url='https://www.indeed.com/cmp/Hubspot', additional_data=None), Result(content='![Banner](https://s4-recruiting.cdn.greenhouse.io/job_board_renderer/job_board_configurations/banners/400/022/800/original/Greenhouse_Banner_2800x600_(1).png?1745339085)\\n\\n[Back to jobs](https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/apolloio)\\n\\n# Senior Product Manager, Expansion\\n\\nRemote, United States\\n\\nApply\\n\\n[Apollo.io](http://apollo.io/) is the leading go-to-market solution for revenue teams, trusted by over 500,000 companies and millions of users globally, from rapidly growing startups to some of the world\\'s largest enterprises. Founded in 2015, the company is one of the fastest growing companies in SaaS, raising approximately $250 million to date and valued at $1.6 billion. [Apollo.io](http://apollo.io/) provides sales and marketing teams with easy access to verified contact data for over 210 million B2B contacts and 35 million companies worldwide, along with tools to engage and convert these contacts in one unified platform. By helping revenue professionals find the most accurate contact information and automating the outreach process, [Apollo.io](http://apollo.io/) turns prospects into customers.\\xa0Apollo raised a series D in 2023 and is backed by top-tier investors, including Sequoia Capital, Bain Capital Ventures, and more, and counts the former President and COO of Hubspot, JD Sherman, among its board members.\\n\\n#### The Role\\n\\nWe\\'re hiring a Senior Product Manager to own customer expansion at Apollo. One of our biggest growth levers is the customers we already have. When paid teams add seats, buy more credits, attach add-ons, or step up to a higher-value plan, that drives compounding revenue. You\\'ll own the in-product surfaces that turn habitual usage into expansion, and you\\'ll make those moments feel obvious, easy, and trusted.\\n\\nOur Product, Design, Engineering teams are structured as Squads. You\\'ll be assigned to a Squad alongside a Product Designer, Data Science partner, an Engineering Manager, and multiple Engineers, and you\\'ll partner closely with the Activation, Free-to-Paid, and Pricing & Packaging teams to keep the monetization experience cohesive end to end.\\n\\n#### What you will own\\n\\n* Team invite and joiner experience that turns every new seat into an active, paying user.\\n* Admin upgrade request flow that captures the moment a teammate asks an admin for more.\\n* Credit and plan upsells that surface at the right time, driven by deeper and more habitual usage.\\n* Hand-offs that confidently guide qualified teams to Apollo\\'s sales team.\\n* Reporting that shows where expansion is healthy and where it is leaking.\\n\\n#### What you will do\\n\\n* **Go deep with paying customers, admins, and end users** to deeply understand their needs, workflows, and friction points. Translate that understanding into the product changes that actually move the needle, and use it to set strategy and own your roadmap.\\n* **Prototype fast.** Use Claude Code and other AI tools to validate ideas before committing engineering resources. Ship to learn, then ship to scale.\\n* **Use AI as a daily multiplier.** Refine your thinking, automate the busywork, keep the team aligned, and free yourself to focus on the highest-leverage work.\\n* **Write clear specs and make hard tradeoffs.** Define the quality bar. Partner with design on experience and with engineering on delivery.\\n* **Measure whether it worked.** Instrument funnels, track conversion and expansion metrics, and feed what you learn back into the next cycle.\\n* **Drive alignment** across Free-to-Paid, Pricing & Packaging, Sales, Support, and other partners so what ships actually lands.\\n\\n#### What we are looking for\\n\\n* **4+ years of proven experience in SaaS product management.** You\\'ve owned products end to end in a SaaS environment and can speak to the outcomes you drove.\\n* **High agency and strong ownership.** You create clarity in ambiguous environments and push work forward without waiting for direction.\\n* **Track record of unlocking revenue.** You\\'ve worked with a team to solve impactful problems that opened up new revenue, and you can point to the result.\\n* **Product judgment.** You focus on the highest-leverage problems, make hard tradeoffs, and say no to good ideas to protect great ones.\\n* **Creative problem solver.** You see the second and third option when most people stop at the first.\\n* **Customer empathy.** You optimize for your u

now](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fronsmiller_salesforce-ceo-marc-benioff-says-ai-wont-activity-7454878278911209474-9ScU&trk=public_post_nav-header-join)\\n\\n[![]()](https://www.linkedin.com/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fronsmiller_salesforce-ceo-marc-benioff-says-ai-wont-activity-7454878278911209474-9ScU&fromSignIn=true&trk=public_post_nav-header-signin)\\n\\n# Benioff on AI replacing entry-level jobs\\n\\nThis title was summarized by AI from the post below.\\n\\n[![View profile for Ron Miller]()](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ronsmiller?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)\\n\\n[Ron Miller](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ronsmiller?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)\\n\\n3w\\n\\nEdited\\n\\n* [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fronsmiller_salesforce-ceo-marc-benioff-says-ai-wont-activity-7454878278911209474-9ScU&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)\\n\\nThis is certainly interesting. Benioff is talking about how AI is replacing jobs, and the jobs that it replaces for starters are the entry level ones. The conundrum for every company though is that you need a base of younger workers because a) they\\'re cheaper and b) they bring youth, enthusiasm and a new way of working into the organization.\\nIf AI requires a certain level of savvy to understand the quality of the answers (and it does in my experience), then you are going to need to build a continually developing workforce or all the people with experience will eventually leave. You will note that Microsoft is encouraging the people with the most experience to take an early retirement package.\\nIf you think from quarter to quarter, as most of these big companies seem to do, you end up with a short-term view. Sure, you can save money by cutting workers. Yes, you can force out the most experienced employees, but who is going to have the institutional knowledge required to judge the quality of the AI, and this is even more important as companies try to switch to agentic workflows.\\nSalesforce may want cheaper labor, but it is also going to get new blood in the process, and that is something every organization actually needs if it wants AI to be more than a one\u2011time cost\u2011cutting exercise.\\n[https://lnkd.in/ehMRvsAy](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FehMRvsAy&urlhash=8sjE&trk=public_post-text)\\n\\n[![Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff Says AI Won\u2019t Kill Entry-Level Jobs and He\u2019s Hiring 1,000 New Grads to Prove It]()\\n\\nSalesforce CEO Marc Benioff Says AI Won\u2019t Kill Entry-Level Jobs and He\u2019s Hiring 1,000 New Grads to Prove It\\n\\nfinance.yahoo.com](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Ffinance%2Eyahoo%2Ecom%2Fsectors%2Ftechnology%2Farticles%2Fsalesforce-ceo-marc-benioff-says-104001400%2Ehtml&urlhash=hWWP&trk=public_post_feed-article-content)\\n\\n[![]()\\n![]()\\n\\n13](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fronsmiller_salesforce-ceo-marc-benioff-says-ai-wont-activity-7454878278911209474-9ScU&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)\\n\\n[7 Comments](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fronsmiller_salesforce-ceo-marc-benioff-says-ai-wont-activity-7454878278911209474-9ScU&trk=public_post_social-actions-comments)\\n\\n[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fronsmiller_salesforce-ceo-marc-benio

How do we build trust in autonomy?\\n* **Technical Orchestration:** Work closely with Engineering and AI Builders to define the architecture for Planning, Memory, and Tool-Use. You\\'ll decide what is the right solution for each hard problem.\\n* **Performance & Reliability:** Agents are notoriously \"vibe-based\" until they aren\\'t.
